steps.title: Extract the slope from line p's equation
steps.content: Line $p$ is $y = 6x - 8$. This is in slope-intercept form where the slope is the coefficient of $x$. Therefore, the slope of line $p$ is \$6$.

steps.title: Use the parallel lines property
steps.content: Parallel lines have the same slope. Since line $s$ is parallel to line $p$, the slope of line $s$ is \$6$.
